content: Date 2026-04-08. Recruiter ping from fintech Klarwave about a senior role ~€92k vs my current €78k. I'm Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer at a Paris logistics startup. Thinking out loud: €92k is +€14k (~18% bump) — about €1,166 gross/month extra, ~€850–900 net/month probably. Klarwave sounded interesting but recruiter didn't mention equity or ML focus. Office in Paris, hybrid 2 days/week. My priorities: move toward Staff/Principal in 12–18 months, do more ML work, and increase savings for family plans in 2 years. Question: is it worth exploring a first call with Klarwave or is the gap too small / the role probably lateral?
Is the quoted €92k gross annual and is that base-only or does it imply total comp including equity/bonus?

Recruiter said "around €92k" — I assume gross/year base, no explicit equity or bonus mentioned. Current is €78k gross. No clear promotion promise at my startup; I could try to negotiate to €85k but nothing committed. I care more about technical scope: if Klarwave is mainly backend fintech work with little ML, it's a dealbreaker. If they offer ML projects, or clear path to Staff and equity, I'm interested. Timeline: recruiter reached out today, 2026-04-08. Should I take an exploratory 30-min call to collect details?
What matters most right now: higher pay, faster path to Staff/Principal, or more ML work?

Priority order: 1) faster path to Staff/Principal within 18 months, 2) ML-heavy work, 3) salary (needs to be at least €95–100k gross or clear equity/promotion roadmap to justify a move). Dealbreakers: no ML focus or no promotion clarity. I can use the call to gather leverage for a counteroffer at my startup. Plan to schedule a call this week. Can you help draft 6 sharp questions to ask the recruiter (neutral exploratory tone)?
Do you want the questions neutral exploratory or slightly signaling that you expect ~€95–100k later?

Neutral exploratory — I want to keep options open and only signal numbers later. Questions should cover compensation (base vs equity), tech/ML weight, team/org, career progression/timeline to Staff, interview loop/timeline, and working logistics (remote/on-call). Now give me 6 concise questions I can read in a 30‑min call.

1) Can you confirm whether €92k is base, and whether there is bonus, equity, or other components (and typical ranges)?
2) What percentage of the role is ML work vs backend/infra, and can you give a couple of current ML projects or use cases?
3) Who will I report to, how big is the team (engineers, data scientists), and what is the tech stack for ML/production?
4) What does the promotion path to Staff/Principal look like at Klarwave, and typical timelines for strong performers?
5) What is the interview loop, expected timeline to offer, and start-date flexibility?
6) What are the hybrid/remote policies, on-call expectations, and key production responsibilities?

Perfect, concise. I'll book the call for this week (aiming before 2026-04-10), keep it exploratory, record answers, and only disclose a €95–100k target if negotiation time comes. If they confirm ML + clear promotion path, I'll proceed; if not, I'll use info to negotiate at my startup.
Good plan — record their answers, note equity details and promotion timelines, and aim to negotiate only after you have a written offer. Want a 1‑line checklist to run through during the call?
